data.title: Fastathon
data.details:
Opportunity Description:

  • Fastathon is a 24-hour online hackathon organized by F.A.S.T., supported by NVIDIA, Belden, and BMW. The event challenges engineering students to develop innovative technology solutions for real-world problems aligned with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and sourced from NGOs and industry.

Competition Format:

Round 1 – SDG Solution PPT Submission:

  • Teams must submit a presentation proposing a technology-based solution addressing a selected Sustainable Development Goal within the given domain.
  • Submissions will be evaluated based on innovation, feasibility, and impact.

Round 2 – Problem Statement Auction:

  • Shortlisted teams will receive a set number of tokens.
  • Teams will participate in a live auction to bid for problem statements based on strategy.

Round 3 – Development & Final Presentation:

  • Teams will develop a working prototype or solution.
  • Participants will present their final solution to the judging panel.
  • Evaluation will be based on innovation, technical implementation, feasibility, and real-world impact.

Rewards:

  • Tokens and points earned during the event can be converted into scores.
  • Participants can use these scores to claim courses from the NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute certification catalogue.
  • These certifications are globally recognized industry certifications.

Eligibility:

  • Open to engineering students only.
  • Team size: 2–4 members.
  • Inter-college teams are allowed.
  • Inter-specialization teams are allowed.

Rules:

  • All participants must register through Unstop.
  • Teams must submit original work.
  • Participants must take part in games and activities conducted during the event to earn additional points.
  • Points earned during the event determine access to NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute certification courses.
  • Plagiarism or unfair practices will lead to disqualification.
  • The decision of the judges will be final and binding.

organisation.name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST), Kattankulathur, Chennai

address_with_country_logo.address: SRM Institute of Science and Technology kattankulathur

data.title: CodeSphere-Innovacion 2026
data.details:
Event Overview:

  • CodeSphere 2026 is an individual Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA) coding battle hosted on the Unstop platform.
  • Participants compete in two elimination rounds, testing problem-solving speed, accuracy, and algorithmic efficiency.

Event Details:

  • Event Name: CodeSphere 2026
  • Format: Individual Participation (No Teams)
  • Platform: Unstop
  • Number of Rounds: 2
  • Duration per Round: 1 Hour 30 Minutes (90 Minutes)
  • Domain: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)

Round Structure & Qualification Rules:

Round 1 — Qualifier Round:

  • Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Total Questions: 4
  • Q1 — Easy
  • Q2 — Medium
  • Q3 — Hard
  • Q4 — Hardest
  • A question is considered completed only when all test cases pass successfully.

Qualification Paths:

  • Standard Path: Complete any 3 out of 4 questions.
  • Alternative Path: Complete Q4 (Hardest) and any 1 other question.

Round 2 — Final Round:

  • Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Total Questions: 3
  • Q1 — Medium
  • Q2 — Hard
  • Q3 — Hardest

Qualification Paths:

  • Standard Path: Complete any 2 out of 3 questions.
  • Alternative Path: Complete Q3 (Hardest) alone.

Scoring System:

Round 1 Scoring:

Easy (Q1)

  • Base Score: 50 points
  • Speed Bonus: +10 points (if solved within first 30 minutes)
  • Maximum Score: 60 points

Medium (Q2)

  • Base Score: 100 points
  • Speed Bonus: +20 points
  • Maximum Score: 120 points

Hard (Q3)

  • Base Score: 150 points
  • Speed Bonus: +30 points
  • Maximum Score: 180 points

Hardest (Q4)

  • Base Score: 200 points
  • Speed Bonus: +40 points
  • Maximum Score: 240 points

Partial Score Formula: (Test Cases Passed ÷ Total Test Cases) × Base Score

Round 2 Scoring:

Medium (Q1)

  • Base Score: 100 points
  • Speed Bonus: +20 points
  • Maximum Score: 120 points

Hard (Q2)

  • Base Score: 175 points
  • Speed Bonus: +35 points
  • Maximum Score: 210 points

Hardest (Q3)

  • Base Score: 250 points
  • Speed Bonus: +50 points
  • Maximum Score: 300 points
  • Partial Score Formula: (Test Cases Passed ÷ Total Test Cases) × Base Score

Scoring Notes:

  • Speed bonus is awarded only if the question is fully solved within the first 30 minutes.
  • Partial scores count toward the total score but do not count for qualification.
  • The total score across both rounds is used for final rankings.

Tie-Breaker Rules:

If participants have equal scores after Round 2, the following criteria apply:

  • Code Efficiency (better time complexity wins)
  • Space Complexity (lower memory usage wins)
  • Submission Time (earlier submission wins)

Academic Integrity & Code of Conduct:

AI Tools Policy:

  • Use of AI-powered tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, or similar assistants is strictly prohibited.
  • Use of AI-based code completion or automated code generation tools is not allowed.
  • Penalty: Immediate and permanent disqualification.

Plagiarism Policy:

  • All code submissions are checked using Unstop plagiarism detection tools and manual review.
  • Copying code from other participants or external sources is prohibited.
  • AI Usage: Immediate disqualification.
  • Plagiarism (First Offence): Formal warning and solution invalidated.
  • Plagiarism (Second Offence): Disqualification from the event.

General Conduct:

  • Participants must not communicate with other participants during the contest.
  • Sharing hints, solutions, or approaches during the round is prohibited.
  • Attempting to manipulate the leaderboard or exploit platform vulnerabilities will result in disqualification.
  • The organizing committee may investigate suspicious submissions.

Platform & Technical Rules:

  • The contest is hosted on Unstop.
  • Participants must register on Unstop before the event.
  • Participants must ensure a stable internet connection during the contest.
  • Supported programming languages will be listed on the Unstop event page.
  • Platform timer on Unstop is the official contest timer.
  • Personal technical issues will not result in time extensions.

Sample Problem Statement Format:

  • Problem Title – Name of the problem
  • Problem Statement – Description of the task
  • Input Format – Description of the input structure
  • Output Format – Description of the required output
  • Constraints – Input size and value limits
  • Sample Input – Example input
  • Sample Output – Expected output for the example
  • Difficulty Tag – Easy / Medium / Hard / Hardest

General Rules:

  • Participation is strictly individual.
  • Participants must be registered on the Unstop platform before the contest begins.
  • Late entries will not be accepted once the round has started.
  • Decisions of the organizing committee are final and binding.
  • Organizers reserve the right to modify rules in exceptional circumstances and will communicate updates on the Unstop event page.

data.title: Brewing Codes 4.0
data.details:
Event Overview:

  • Brewing Codes 4.0 – Hackathon 2026 is a 24-hour national hackathon organized by the Maharishi School of Engineering & Technology (MSoET), Maharishi University of Information Technology (MUIT), Noida. The event is conducted in collaboration with IQAC and in association with upGrad, ICC, MIIF, and Samatrix.
  • The hackathon brings together innovators, developers, and problem-solvers from across the country to develop impactful solutions for real-world challenges through collaboration, creativity, and technical expertise.

Tracks:

Teams will work on one of the following themes:

  • AI in Healthcare and Digital Well-Being
  • Agentic AI and Autonomous Systems
  • Cybersecurity, Digital Privacy and Trust
  • Sustainable Technology and Circular Economy
  • Smart Cities and Urban Innovation
  • Low Code or No Code Platforms for Rapid Application Development

Team Size:

  • Minimum team size: 2 members
  • Maximum team size: 4 members
  • All team members must belong to the same institution

Participation Fee:

  • No registration fee
  • Participation in the hackathon is completely free

Prizes And Rewards:

Total prize pool: Up to ₹80,000

  • Winner Team: ₹40,000 and winner's certificate
  • Runner-up Team: ₹20,000 and runner-up certificate
  • Student Innovation: ₹20,000 (2 teams will receive ₹10,000 each) and certificates
  • All participants will receive official hackathon participation certificates

Venue:

  • Maharishi School of Engineering & Technology (MSoET)
  • Maharishi University of Information Technology (MUIT), Noida Campus

Important Dates:

  • Last date of registration: 24 March 2026
  • Round 1: 27 March 2026
  • Round 2 and finals: 28 March 2026

Competition Guidelines:

  • Teams must bring their own laptops, software, and necessary development tools
  • Teams must select an appropriate track and prepare a structured idea document for presentation
  • No new members can be added or removed after registration
  • Judges’ decisions will be final and binding

Competition Rules:

  • Each team must consist of 2 to 4 members, including a team leader
  • All team members must be from the same institution
  • A faculty mentor is allowed but will not be permitted inside the competition room
  • Team composition cannot be modified once registration is completed

Competition Structure:

  • Round 1 – 27 March 2026
  • Venue: Maharishi University of Information Technology, Noida Campus
  • Duration: 6 hours

Submission requirements:

Each team must submit a PowerPoint presentation with a maximum of 5 slides including:

  • Team name, leader and members, institution name
  • Problem statement and idea description
  • Solution, prototype, and technology used
  • Use cases and applicability

Judging and evaluation:

  • Teams will be evaluated based on predefined evaluation rubrics
  • Results will be announced at the end of the day
  • Teams not qualifying for Round 2 will receive participation certificates

Round 2 – 28 March 2026

  • Only top-performing teams from Round 1 will qualify for Round 2
  • Selected teams will be allowed to stay overnight on campus to refine their projects
  • Final evaluation will determine the winner and runner-up teams

Add-On Facilities And Support:

Infrastructure and technical support:

  • High-speed WiFi across the venue
  • Power backup and charging stations
  • Dedicated workstations and comfortable seating
  • On-site IT support for technical assistance

Logistics and hospitality:

  • Refreshments and meals provided at scheduled intervals
  • Overnight stay facility for teams qualifying for Round 2
  • First aid and medical assistance available
  • 24/7 security to ensure a safe environment

Presentation and evaluation support:

  • Projectors and screens for team presentations
  • Expert guidance for PPT and documentation review
  • Transparent judging criteria

Additional benefits:

  • Media and social media coverage for winning teams
  • Participation certificates for all registered participants

Event Schedule:

27 March 2026 – Round 1

  • 09:00 AM – Reporting time for teams
  • 09:30 AM – Inauguration and welcome messages
  • 10:00 AM – Hackathon begins
  • 04:00 PM – Round 1 project evaluation
  • 05:30 PM – Round 1 results announcement
  • 05:45 PM – Round 2 begins

28 March 2026 – Round 2 and Finals

  • 09:30 AM – Final evaluation
  • 12:30 PM – Winners announcement and valedictory session

organisation.name: Maharishi University of Information Technology (MUIT), NOIDA

data.title: Prayacthon 2026
data.details:
The upcoming hackathon, conducted by Tekion, is an exciting opportunity for students to showcase their creativity, technical knowledge, and problem-solving skills. The event aims to bring together passionate innovators who are eager to collaborate, learn, and build impactful solutions within a limited time. Through this hackathon, participants will experience a fast-paced environment that encourages experimentation, teamwork, and innovative thinking.

 

The event provides a platform for students to explore new ideas, apply their technical skills, and develop practical solutions to real-world challenges. Participants will be encouraged to think critically, experiment with different approaches, and transform their ideas into working prototypes. By working together in teams, students will not only strengthen their technical abilities but also enhance their communication, collaboration, and time-management skills.

 

Key Guidelines

 

• Team Size: Each team must consist of 2–4 participants, allowing effective collaboration while ensuring that every member contributes actively to the project.

 

• Inter-College Participation: Teams may include members from different colleges, encouraging broader networking and collaboration among students from various institutions.

 

• Inter-Specialization Teams: Participants from different academic specializations are allowed to form teams together. This promotes interdisciplinary innovation and allows teams to combine different strengths such as programming, design, analytics, and strategic thinking.

 

• Problem Statements: The problem statements will be released shortly before the event begins. Once announced, teams must analyze the challenge, brainstorm ideas, and start building their solutions within the given timeframe.

 

• Event Duration: The hackathon will take place from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM, providing participants with an intensive eight-hour window to conceptualize, design, and develop their solutions.

 

During the event, teams will work collaboratively to design innovative solutions based on the problem statements provided. Participants will divide responsibilities among team members, brainstorm creative approaches, and focus on building practical and efficient solutions. The time-bound nature of the hackathon encourages quick thinking, adaptability, and effective teamwork.

 

Beyond building a solution, the hackathon also provides participants with valuable exposure to industry-level challenges and expectations. Students will gain hands-on experience in ideation, development, and presentation while working alongside peers who share similar interests in technology and innovation.

 

Evaluation

 

The final projects will be evaluated by a panel of 4–5 judges, all of whom are industry experts. Each team will present their solution and explain their approach to solving the problem. The judges will assess the projects based on factors such as innovation, feasibility, technical implementation, and overall impact.

 

Overall, this hackathon conducted by Tekion promises to be a dynamic and engaging experience where students can test their abilities, collaborate with talented peers, and demonstrate their potential to industry professionals.

data.title: Code Uncode 2026
data.details:
Guidelines:

  • This is an individual event.
  • This event is only for students pursuing their Undergrad or Postgrad.

Rules:

  • All participants are supposed to register and join the designated Whatsapp groups. Official communication from the organizers shall be posted there.
  • Prelims shall be conducted in an Online Format. The platform and the details shall be conveyed to you soon, however it is expected that all participants shall maintain integrity and honesty of the examination. Any violations or cheating shall lead to immediate disqualification.
  • Regionals shall be conducted at 3 centres across Mumbai and Pune and all participants can attend at most 2 of the Regionals. Physical attendance is mandatory and absence may lead to elimination. The best performance shall be considered. Top 90 (30 + 30 + 30) shall move forward to the finals.
  • Some centres may expect Regionalists to carry their devices, whereas some centres shall provide devices. In their case, Regionalists shall be intimated beforehand. 
  • Finals shall be conducted at SVKM's DJ Sanghvi College of Engineering, Mumbai. 
  • The duration of Prelims and Regionals shall be 2 hours whereas the duration of the Finals shall be 3 hours.

WhatsApp group link for interested participants here.

Roundwise Details:

Prelims:

  • Date: 22nd March, 2026. 
  • Time: 20:05 p.m. 
  • Location: Online

Regionals:

  • Each participant can register for up to 2 regionals and if qualified, based on their preferences and the regional capacity, will be allowed to the regional.
  • The Regional Round will be held offline at 3 locations:
    • College of Engineering, Pune (CoEP)
    • Dwarkadas J. Sanghvi College of Engineering
    • Sardar Patel Institute of Technology

Schedule:

  • College of Engineering, Pune (CoEP)
    • Date: 29th March, 2026. (Saturday)
  • Sardar Patel Institute of Technology
    • Date: 30th March, 2026. (Monday)
  • Dwarkadas J. Sanghvi College of Engineering
    • Date: 1st April, 2026. (Wednesday)

Finals

  • The Top 30 participants who have not already qualified for the finals will be qualified for the finals from each Regional round
  • Date: 11th April, 2026. (Sunday)
  • Location: Dwarkadas J. Sanghvi College of Engineering

data.title: HackToFuture 4.0
data.details:
HackToFuture 4.0 brings together students who love building, experimenting, and solving real-world problems through technology. It's a space where ideas are shared, teams collabrate, and creativity meets coding. 

During the hackathon, participants will work together to design and develop solutions to meaningful challenges. It's not just writing code, it's about thinking differently, learning from others and turning ideas into something real.

Whether you're a developer, designer, or someone who enjoys solving problems, HackToFuture 4.0 is the perfect opportunity to build, learn and be part of an exciting hackathon experience.

Who Can Participate?

  • Open to undergraduate students pursuing BE/BTech, BCA/MCA, BSc (CS), and other relevant degrees.
  • Participants must register as a team of 2 to 4 members.
  • Teams can be interdepartmental, inter-semester.

Problem Statements will be released soon.

General Rules:

  • Each team must nominate a team leader who will serve as the main point of contact.
  • Participants must bring their own laptops, chargers, and any additional accessories required.
  • Teams must strictly adhere to the given problem statements.
  • Submissions must be original, and teams should be prepared to justify their work during evaluations.
  • Judges’ decisions will be final and binding, with any disputes resolved at the discretion of the organizing committee.

On-Campus Stay & Facilities:

To foster collaboration, innovation, and an immersive experience, all participants must stay on campus for the entire duration of the event. Leaving the premises during the hackathon is not permitted.

  • Accommodation will be provided on campus for all participants, ensuring a comfortable and hassle-free stay.
  • Meals and snacks will be provided at scheduled intervals to keep participants energized and focused.
  • Basic amenities and necessary facilities will be arranged to create a productive environment.
  • Participants are encouraged to bring any additional personal items they may require during their stay.

Code of Conduct:

HackToFuture 4.0 is built on the principles of integrity, inclusivity, and fair competition. All participants are expected to:

  • Maintain professionalism and respect towards fellow participants, mentors, and organizers.
  • Follow ethical coding practices and avoid any form of misconduct.
  • Cooperate with the organizing team and adhere to all event rules and schedules.

By participating in HackToFuture 4.0, teams agree to abide by these guidelines and contribute to a smooth, fair, and enjoyable experience for everyone.

data.title: La Casa De Code
data.details:
La Casa De Code is a 24-hour innovation marathon organized by DSU-ACM, Department of Computer Science & Engineering, Dayananda Sagar University – Harohalli Campus as part of TechFlix Season 2.

The event is designed for builders, developers, designers, and problem-solvers who enjoy transforming ideas into working prototypes under intense time constraints. Participants will collaborate in teams to develop impactful technology solutions addressing real-world challenges across domains like AI & ML, Cybersecurity, FinTech, HealthTech, Sustainability, Web3, and DevTools.

During the 24-hour hackathon, teams will ideate, design, and build a functional prototype while receiving mentorship, guidance, and feedback from industry experts and experienced developers. The event encourages creativity, innovation, and practical implementation while fostering collaboration and learning.

Rules & Guidelines

  • All participants must register through the official registration platform.
  • Teams can consist of 1 to 3 members only.
  • Participants must select a track or build under Open Innovation.
  • Teams must build their solution within the 24-hour hackathon window.
  • Use of open-source libraries, APIs, and frameworks is allowed.
  • Pre-built projects are not allowed; however, basic templates or frameworks may be used.
  • All projects must be submitted before the code freeze deadline.
  • Teams must provide:
    • Source code repository (GitHub/GitLab)
    • Project demo or working prototype
    • Brief documentation explaining the solution
  • Any form of plagiarism, unfair practices, or rule violations will lead to disqualification.
  • Judges’ decisions will be final and binding.

Eligibility:

  • Open to undergraduate and postgraduate students from any recognized college or university.
  • Participants from all technical backgrounds such as Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Electronics, and related fields are welcome.
  • Students from any year of study can participate.
  • Participants must register individually or as a team of maximum three members.

Hackathon Tracks

Participants can build solutions under the following tracks:

  • Open Innovation – Build any impactful technology solution solving a real-world problem.
  • AI: The Submission That Can't Lie – AI system for verifying claims in pitch decks and submissions.
  • DeFi: Credit for the Creditless – Blockchain-based alternative credit scoring and micro-loans.
  • GenAI × GTM – AI-powered marketing campaign generator and analytics engine.
  • Cybersecurity: Moving Target Defense – Automated dynamic defense systems for network security.
  • Agent Arena – AI agents competing to autonomously build products.
  • Behavioural Analysis – On-device behavioral insights platform similar to a “digital personality mirror.”
  • Utility Tokenization – No-code token creation platform for communities and organizations.

Event Format

  • Stage 1 – Registration: Participants register individually or as teams via the registration portal.
  • Stage 2 – Hackathon Begins: Teams will start building their solutions once the hackathon begins.
  • Stage 3 – Mentor Round; Mentors will interact with teams, review ideas, and provide guidance.
  • Stage 4 – Evaluation Round 1: Teams present their progress and receive feedback to improve their projects.
  • Stage 5 – Final Submission: Teams submit their final prototype along with documentation and repository.
  • Stage 6 – Final Demo & Judging: Teams will present their project demo to the judging panel.
  • Stage 7 – Winners Announcement: Top teams will be selected based on judging criteria.

Judging Criteria:

Projects will be evaluated based on:

  • Innovation & Creativity
  • Technical Implementation
  • Real-World Impact
  • UI/UX & Design
  • Clarity of Presentation and Demo

Rewards & Recognition

Total Prize Pool: ₹1,50,000

  • 1st Place: ₹75,000
  • 2nd Place: ₹45,000
  • 3rd Place: ₹30,000

Additional benefits may include:

  • Swag kits
  • Participation certificates
  • Internship and networking opportunities
  • Potential incubation support for promising projects

Event Details

  • Date: 16–17 April 2026
  • Duration: 24 Hours
  • Location: DSU Harohalli Campus
  • Team Size: 1–3 members

data.title: Future Front 2026 – National Level Symposium on Emerging Computing Technologies
data.details:
Future Front 2026 – A National Level Symposium:

  • Future Front is a National Level Symposium organized at SOE DSU Main Campus on 16 April 2026, bringing together students, researchers, and technology enthusiasts from across the country to explore the latest trends and innovations in computing technologies.
  • The symposium provides a platform for participants to present research ideas, share innovative solutions, and discuss emerging technological advancements in areas such as Artificial Intelligence, Cybersecurity, Robotics, and Internet of Things.
  • The event aims to promote knowledge exchange, research culture, and collaboration among young innovators, helping shape the future of advanced computing technologies.
  • Participants will submit original research papers, and shortlisted teams will present their work before a panel of judges.

Event Format (Online + Offline Participation):

  • Although the event is listed as Online on the platform, Future Front will be conducted in a Hybrid format.

Paper Submission Round – Online:

  • All participants must submit their research paper through the platform.

Final Presentation Round – Hybrid Mode:

  • Shortlisted teams will present their research in one of the following modes:

Offline Presentation:

  • Participants from Bangalore or nearby regions are encouraged to present at SOE DSU Main Campus.

Online Presentation:

  • Participants from outside Bangalore may present their research through a video conferencing platform (Google Meet / Zoom).
  • Each team will be given 8–10 minutes to present their research followed by a Q&A session with the judges.

Team Size:

  • Participants can register in teams of 3–4 members per team.

Eligibility & Conditions:

  • Participants must be undergraduate or postgraduate students from recognized institutions.
  • The submitted research paper must be original work and must not have been published or submitted to any other conference or symposium.
  • The research topic must be relevant to the symposium themes.
  • Papers must follow academic integrity standards.
  • The plagiarism limit must not exceed 10–15% similarity.
  • Teams must ensure that at least one member is available for the final presentation round.

Symposium Tracks:

  • Participants can submit papers under the following tracks:
    • Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
    • Robotics & Intelligent Automation
    • Cybersecurity & Digital Forensics
    • Internet of Things (IoT) & Smart Systems
    • Open Innovation

Paper Submission Guidelines:

  • Participants must submit their research paper in PDF format following the standard research paper structure.

Title Page:

  • The first page must include:
    • Title of the Paper
    • Author Name(s)
    • Institution / College Name
    • Email Address

Abstract (150–250 Words):

  • The abstract should provide a concise summary including:
    • The problem being addressed
    • The methodology or approach used
    • The expected results or outcomes
    • The significance of the research

Keywords:

  • Provide 4–6 keywords representing the main concepts of the research paper.

Introduction:

  • Introduce the research topic by including:
    • Background information
    • Statement of the problem
    • Objectives or purpose of the study

Literature Review:

  • Discuss related work including:
    • Overview of existing research
    • Identification of research gaps

Methodology:

  • Explain how the research was conducted, including:
    • Research design or approach
    • Tools, technologies, or techniques used
    • Data sources or datasets utilized

Results / Analysis:

  • Present the outcomes of the research including:
    • Key findings
    • Data analysis or observations

Discussion:

  • Interpret and explain the results including:
    • Meaning and significance of findings
    • Potential impact and implications of the study

Conclusion & Future Work:

  • Summarize the research and suggest future improvements including:
    • Key conclusions from the study
    • Possible future research directions

References:

  • All cited works must be properly referenced using IEEE or APA citation format.

data.title: Cosmix
data.details:
Cosmix – Innovating Beyond Boundaries

  • The rapid advancement of technology is transforming industries and creating new possibilities for innovation. Cosmix is a 36-hour hackathon organized by the Student Satellite Group and Google Developer Groups (GDG) of IIIT Sri City, in collaboration with GalaxEye, aimed at bringing together passionate developers, designers, and innovators to build impactful technology solutions.
  • This hackathon provides a platform for students to explore cutting-edge technologies, collaborate with talented peers, and transform innovative ideas into real-world solutions. Participants will have the opportunity to develop projects across domains such as Artificial Intelligence, Cloud Computing, Web and Mobile Development, Data Analytics, Space Technology, and other emerging technologies.
  • Through this event, participants will be encouraged to think creatively, solve challenging real-world problems, and build innovative prototypes that demonstrate technical excellence and practical impact.
  • Cosmix aims to foster innovation, collaboration, and hands-on learning, empowering students to push the boundaries of technology and create solutions that can contribute to the future of digital and space-driven advancements.
  • Whether you're a beginner exploring your first hackathon or an experienced developer eager to build something groundbreaking, Cosmix serves as the perfect launchpad to learn, build, and innovate.

Build the future. Innovate beyond limits.

Rewards & Opportunities:

Prize Pool: ₹50,000

Top-performing teams will be awarded prizes from a total prize pool of ₹50,000.

Internship Opportunities:

Outstanding participants may also receive probable internship opportunities and networking prospects with industry professionals through our collaborating partners.

Eligibility:

  • Open to Undergraduate and Postgraduate students from any recognized institution.
  • Participants must register through Unstop before the registration deadline.
  • Each team must consist of 2–4 members.

Team Size:

  • Minimum: 2 members
  • Maximum: 4 members

Rules & Guidelines:

  • Each team must have 2–4 members.
  • Participants must carry their valid college ID cards during the event.
  • Teams must submit their Round 1 presentation before the deadline.
  • Shortlisted teams will be notified through Unstop or email.
  • All projects must be original work developed during the hackathon.
  • The use of open-source libraries, APIs, and frameworks is allowed, but proper attribution must be provided.
  • Any form of plagiarism or unfair practices will result in immediate disqualification.
  • Teams must submit their final code, presentation, and demo before the final deadline.
  • The decision of the judging panel will be final.

Judging Criteria

Projects will be evaluated based on:

  • Innovation and Creativity
  • Technical Implementation
  • Problem Relevance and Impact
  • Feasibility and Scalability
  • Quality of Presentation and Demonstration

data.title: DevGathering 2K26
data.details:
Welcome to the DevGathering 2k26 - by MLSA MIET !

DevGathering 2K26 is the flagship technical, 36-Hours hackathon organized by the Microsoft Learn Student Ambassadors (MLSA) Community at MIET Meerut. The event brings together passionate developers, designers, and innovators to collaborate, compete, and create impactful solutions. With multiple exciting tracks, hands-on challenges, and an engaging hackathon environment, participants get the opportunity to showcase their technical skills, learn from peers, and build innovative projects while competing for an exciting prize pool.

 

About the Opportunity :

Participants will get the opportunity to work on innovative ideas, build functional prototypes, and present their solutions before a panel of judges. The event aims to foster creativity, teamwork, and practical learning while providing exposure to industry-relevant technologies.

 

This hackathon is your chance to showcase your skills in Teamwork and Collaboration, Adaptability, Problem Solving, and Critical Thinking. Whether you're looking to build something groundbreaking or refine your existing abilities, DevGathering 2k26 offers a platform for growth and innovation within the Software Development domain.

 

Registration Process :

  • Scroll down to the Attachments section
  • You'll find the guide to create ASI One Usernames and after creating each member of the team have to fill the google form. 
  • This is a mandatory step, failing to fill the google form may result in disqualification of the team.
  • Then while registering on unstop the team leader have to fill the ASI One usernames of each team member.
  • And then after completing you can submit your PPT for the first round.

 

Themes/Problem Statements :

Tracks are available on the website devgathering2k26.xyz . 
Anyone can take reference from them or can choose miscellaneous topics.

 

Eligibility and Registration:

  •  Participants can only register as a team
     Team size: 2–4 member
  • Open to all undergraduate, postgraduate and working professionals.
  • Students from any specialization are eligible to participate.
  •  Inter-specialization teams are allowed.
  •  Inter-college participation is allowed.

 

Hackathon format :

  • Participants will choose a problem statement from the available tracks or by themselves.
  • Teams will submit a ppt for shortlisting and they have to prepare a prototype till hackathon day, once shortlisted.
  • Final submissions for offline round 1 will include a working prototype and project presentation.
  • Shortlisted teams for the final round will present their solutions to the judging panel.

Duration :

  • Total duration: 36 hours (Hackathon), only eligible if shortlisted

 

Join the DevGathering 2k26 hackathon at MIET and be part of an unforgettable offline experience!

organisation.name: Meerut Institute of Engineering and Technology, (MIET)

data.title: BuildWithAI – 24 Hour International Innovation Hackathon
data.details:
Event Overview:

  • INNOVATEX 4.0 – BuildWithAI is a 24-hour International Innovation Hackathon organized at Presidency University, Bengaluru.
  • It is a global AI-focused hackathon where students design and build working prototypes using Artificial Intelligence technologies to solve real-world problems.
  • The event allows hybrid participation, enabling teams to join either offline at the venue or online from anywhere in the world.

Event Details:

  • Event Name: BuildWithAI – 24 Hour International Innovation Hackathon
  • Event Type: Global AI Hackathon
  • Mode: Hybrid (Offline + Online International Participation)
  • Venue: Presidency University, Bengaluru
  • Duration: 24 Hours
  • Participation: Student Teams (Global Participation)

Objective:

  • Participants will develop a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) powered by Artificial Intelligence that addresses real-world challenges.

Projects may include:

  • Web applications
  • Mobile applications
  • AI-powered platforms
  • Automation systems
  • Intelligent tools or digital solutions

Final solutions should demonstrate:

  • Innovation and creativity
  • Technical implementation
  • AI integration
  • Real-world usability and impact

Team Composition:

  • Only students are allowed to participate.
  • Minimum team size: 1 member
  • Maximum team size: 4 members
  • Teams may include students from different universities or countries.
  • Interdisciplinary teams are encouraged.

Example Team Formats:

  • 4 students
  • 3 students
  • 2 students
  • 1 student (Solo participant)

International Participation:

  • Students from any university worldwide may participate.

Teams can join:

  • Offline at Presidency University
  • Online through virtual participation

Important guidelines:

  • All schedules follow IST (Indian Standard Time).
  • Online teams must ensure stable internet connectivity for presentations.
  • Online participants will submit projects digitally and present virtually.

Development Guidelines:

Participants may use:

  • Any programming language
  • Any development framework
  • AI tools, APIs, or platforms
  • Pre-trained AI models

Supported AI platforms include:

  • OpenAI / ChatGPT
  • Google Gemini
  • HuggingFace
  • TensorFlow / PyTorch
  • LangChain and other AI APIs

Important rules:

  • Pre-built complete projects are not allowed.
  • Previously developed code cannot be reused.
  • All development must occur during the 24-hour hackathon period.

Teams should:

  • Maintain a GitHub repository for the project.
  • Document the system architecture and AI implementation.
  • Clearly explain the workflow and system design.

Hackathon Structure:

Phase 1 – Idea Submission

Teams must submit:

  • Problem statement
  • Proposed AI solution
  • Expected impact
  • Feasibility overview

Phase 2 – Development Phase

Teams work on:

  • System architecture
  • AI model or API integration
  • Prototype or MVP development

Mentors may review progress during the hackathon.

Phase 3 – Final Demonstration

Teams must submit:

  • Working prototype or MVP
  • GitHub repository
  • Presentation slides
  • Live demonstration or explanation

Judging Criteria:

  • Innovation & Creativity: 25%
  • AI Integration & Technical Depth: 25%
  • Functionality & Execution: 20%
  • Scalability & Practical Impact: 20%
  • Presentation & Clarity: 10%

Total Score: 100 Marks

General Rules:

  • Participants must follow the instructions provided by organizers and mentors.
  • Ethical and responsible use of AI is mandatory.
  • Offensive, harmful, or inappropriate content will lead to disqualification.
  • All deliverables must be submitted before the deadline.
  • Judges’ decisions are final and binding.
  • Offline participants must carry valid student ID cards.

Code Of Conduct:

  • Participants are expected to maintain professional behaviour throughout the hackathon.

Expected conduct includes:

  • Respect towards organizers, judges, mentors, and participants
  • Fair collaboration and healthy competition
  • Honest and ethical project development

The following actions will lead to disqualification:

  • Plagiarism
  • Misconduct or harassment
  • Violation of rules
  • Unauthorized use of external work

Certification And Recognition:

  • Participation certificates will be provided to all valid participants.

Official Note:

  • This rule book serves as the official guideline for the BuildWithAI – 24 Hour International Innovation Hackathon under INNOVATEX 4.0 at Presidency University, Bengaluru.
  • All announcements and updates will be communicated through the official event channels.

data.title: HackOPhobia by CampusCrew
data.details:
HackOPhobia is a high-energy, 3-hour “Build Anything” Mini Hackathon designed to challenge creativity, speed, and real-world problem-solving under pressure.

Organized by the Coding Club of Chandigarh University – Uttar Pradesh Campus, HackOPhobia is proudly powered and supported by CampusCrew, in collaboration with Unstop, MLH (Major League Hacking), and Google Gemini.

Date: 16th March 2026
Time: 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM
Venue: Chandigarh University, Uttar Pradesh Campus

What Makes HackOPhobia Different?

Unlike traditional hackathons that stretch across 24–48 hours, HackOPhobia compresses innovation into just 3 intense hours. Participants must:

  • Think fast
  • Build smart
  • Validate quickly
  • Deliver a working MVP

Whether it's an AI-powered tool, a productivity app, a web platform, an automation solution, or something completely unexpected — you build it, you pitch it.

With support from Google Gemini, participants are encouraged to integrate AI capabilities, explore rapid prototyping, and push the boundaries of intelligent product development.

Why Participate?

  • Hands-on real-time building experience
  • Exposure to modern AI tools and APIs
  • Networking with builders & innovators
  • Sponsor goodies, snacks & refreshments
  • Certificates powered by MLH & Unstop recognition
  • A platform to showcase your innovation in front of mentors and evaluators

The Mission:

HackOPhobia is more than just a hackathon — it’s a movement to eliminate the “phobia” of building. Many students hesitate to start because they fear failure, complexity, or judgment.

This event is about:

  • Taking initiative
  • Breaking hesitation
  • Shipping fast
  • Building with confidence

Because innovation doesn’t wait.

data.title: Global Prompt Engineering Challenge
data.details:
Event Overview:

  • From Ideas to AI Creations: Global Prompt Engineering Challenge is an international technical competition organized by the MakerSpace Cluster at Presidency University, Bengaluru under INNOVATEX 4.0.
  • The competition evaluates participants’ ability to design structured prompts for Generative AI systems to solve real-world problems effectively.
  • Teams will develop, test, and refine prompts that generate high-quality AI outputs, demonstrating strong prompt architecture, contextual reasoning, optimization, and ethical AI usage.

Event Details:

  • Event Name: From Ideas to AI Creations – Global Prompt Engineering Challenge
  • Organizing Body: MakerSpace Cluster, Presidency University
  • Event Type: International Prompt Engineering Competition
  • Mode: Hybrid (Offline + International Participation)
  • Team Size: Maximum 4 members
  • Duration: 6 Hours + Final Presentation
  • Event Date: 7 April
  • Venue: Hybrid (Offline at Presidency University + Online Participation)

Event Objective:

Participants will transform real-world problem statements into well-structured prompts capable of producing optimized AI-generated outputs.

The challenge focuses on:

  • Prompt architecture and engineering
  • Contextual reasoning
  • AI output optimization
  • Ethical AI usage
  • Creative problem solving using AI systems

Possible problem domains include:

  • Healthcare
  • Education
  • Business Intelligence
  • Software Development
  • Sustainability
  • Social Impact

Teams will design, test, iterate, and refine prompts before presenting their final solutions to a jury panel.

Technical Specifications:

Prompt Design Requirements

Participants must design prompts that clearly define:

  • Objective
  • Context
  • Constraints
  • Instructions
  • Expected output format

Prompt Structure Components

Each prompt should include:

  • Context definition
  • Clear task instructions
  • Constraints and conditions
  • Output formatting requirements

Technology Stack

Participants may use any permitted Generative AI tools, including:

  • ChatGPT
  • Claude
  • Gemini
  • Perplexity
  • Open-source LLM platforms

Iteration and Refinement:

Teams must demonstrate multiple iterations of prompt optimization that improve output quality.

Output Quality Requirements

AI-generated outputs should demonstrate:

  • Relevance
  • Accuracy
  • Clarity
  • Logical reasoning
  • Alignment with the problem statement

Ethical AI Compliance

Participants must ensure:

  • No harmful or biased outputs
  • No unethical prompt engineering
  • Responsible AI usage

Documentation Requirements

Teams must document:

  • Prompt architecture
  • Iteration methodology
  • Optimization reasoning
  • Final AI outputs

General Participation Guidelines:

Participants must:

  • Follow the official event schedule
  • Bring their own laptops and chargers
  • Conduct all prompt engineering during the event duration
  • Ensure active contribution from all team members

Teams must document:

  • Selected problem statement
  • Prompt engineering approach
  • Iteration improvements
  • AI-generated outputs

Additional rules:

  • High-speed internet and power backup will be provided by organizers
  • Only publicly available AI tools may be used
  • Plagiarism of prompts, solutions, or ideas will result in disqualification
  • Participants must wear official event ID badges at all times

Safety And Conduct Rules:

Participants must maintain professional behaviour.

Strictly prohibited activities include:

  • Harassment or offensive behaviour
  • Use of illegal substances
  • Damage to university property
  • Any unethical activity during the competition

Violations may result in immediate disqualification.

Event Structure:

Challenge Briefing

  • Problem statement briefing and guidelines
  • Duration: 30 Minutes

Prompt Engineering Phase

  • Teams design and optimize prompts
  • Duration: 4.5 Hours

Final Presentation

  • Teams present their solution to the jury
  • Duration: 2 Hours

Jury Evaluation & Awards

  • Result announcement and closing ceremony
  • Duration: 30 Minutes

Submission Guidelines:

Teams must submit one consolidated PDF report containing:

  • Selected problem statement
  • Objective of the prompt
  • Final engineered prompt(s)
  • Prompt structure explanation
  • Summary of AI-generated outputs
  • Iteration log demonstrating improvements

Additionally:

  • Teams must prepare a presentation pitch deck for the jury evaluation.
  • Submissions must be uploaded through the designated competition platform before the deadline.

Judging Criteria:

  • Prompt Design and Structure: 25%
  • Output Relevance and Accuracy: 25%
  • Creativity and Innovation: 20%
  • Prompt Optimization and Refinement: 15%
  • Ethical and Responsible AI Usage: 10%
  • Presentation and Explanation: 5%

Scoring Overview:

  • Prompt Design: 25 Points
  • Output Accuracy: 25 Points
  • Creativity: 20 Points
  • Optimization: 15 Points
  • Ethical AI Usage: 10 Points
  • Presentation: 5 Points

Total Score: 100 Points

Penalties And Disqualification:

  • Late Submission: −10 Points
  • Plagiarism: Immediate Disqualification
  • Unethical Conduct: Permanent Ban
  • Offensive Behaviour: Immediate Disqualification
  • External Development: Immediate Disqualification
  • Tampering with Other Teams: Immediate Disqualification

Awards And Recognition:

  • 1st Prize: ₹10,000
  • 2nd Prize: ₹7,000
  • 3rd Prize: ₹5,000

Total Prize Pool: ₹22,000

All participants will receive International Participation Certificates.

Event Coordinators:

  • Faculty Coordinator: Ms. Shana Aneevan – CAI – shana.a@presidencyuniversity.in
  • Faculty Coordinator: Mr. R. Josephine – ISE – josephine.r@presidencyuniversity.in
  • Event Lead: Devanandana S – Student – devananadana.2023cai0126@presidencyuniversity.in

General Instructions:

  • Follow all Presidency University and MakerSpace Cluster policies.
  • Respect judges, organizers, and fellow participants.
  • Plagiarism or idea reuse will lead to disqualification.
  • Judges’ decisions are final and binding.
  • Organizers may showcase selected solutions after the event.

Official Note:

  • This rule book serves as the official international guideline for the Global Prompt Engineering Challenge under INNOVATEX 4.0 at Presidency University, Bengaluru.
  • All updates and announcements will be communicated through official event channels and the MakerSpace community.

data.title: Pixel Craft Global UI/UX Design Challenge
data.details:
Event Overview:

  • Pixel Craft – Global UI/UX Product Design Challenge is an international design hackathon organized by the MakerSpace Cluster at Presidency University, Bengaluru under INNOVATEX 4.0.
  • The event challenges participants to design innovative digital product experiences by applying UI/UX principles, user-centered design, and product thinking.
  • Participants will work under time constraints to create wireframes, user flows, and high-fidelity interface designs that solve real-world problems.

Event Details:

  • Event Name: Pixel Craft – Global UI/UX Product Design Challenge
  • Organized By: MakerSpace Cluster, Presidency University
  • Event Type: International Design Hackathon
  • Mode: Hybrid (Offline + Online International Participation)
  • Team Size: 1–4 Members
  • Duration: 6 Hours + Final Presentation

Event Objective:

Participants will analyze user needs and design digital interfaces that demonstrate:

  • Human-centered design
  • Accessibility and usability
  • Visual design consistency
  • Product thinking and innovation

The challenge simulates real-world product design workflows, encouraging collaboration, creativity, and strategic thinking.

Technical Specifications:

Platform and Tools

All designs must be created using:

  • Figma (mandatory design platform)

Participants may use:

  • Figma design tools
  • Vector graphics tools within Figma
  • Self-created UI components

Restricted Components

The following are strictly prohibited:

  • Pre-built UI kits
  • Pre-designed templates
  • AI-generated UI designs
  • Copying existing product interfaces
  • Importing external assets created before the event

All design work must be created from scratch during the event duration.

International Participation Rules:

To support global participants:

  • Teams from any country or university may participate.
  • Online participants must submit designs through a Figma link before the deadline.
  • Presentations may be conducted virtually via video conference.
  • All schedules follow IST (Indian Standard Time).
  • Teams must ensure stable internet connectivity during final presentations.

General Guidelines:

Participants must:

  • Bring their own laptops or devices
  • Follow the official event schedule
  • Work only during the official competition window
  • Maintain professional and ethical conduct

Teams must document:

  • Problem statement
  • Design approach
  • UI/UX process followed
  • Key design decisions

Safety And Professional Conduct:

  • Participants must maintain a safe and respectful environment.

Strictly prohibited activities include:

  • Harassment or discrimination
  • Academic misconduct or plagiarism
  • Use of unauthorized software
  • Disrupting other teams

Violations will result in immediate disqualification.

Event Structure:

Design Sprint Phase 1

  • Activities: Problem analysis, user research, wireframing
  • Duration: 3 Hours

Design Sprint Phase 2

  • Activities: High-fidelity UI design and user flows
  • Duration: 3 Hours

Final Jury Presentation

  • Design explanation and Q&A
  • Duration: 10 Minutes per team

Submission Guidelines:

Each team must submit:

Figma Design Link:

The design must include:

  • Wireframes
  • UI screens
  • User flow navigation

Presentation Deck (PDF or PPT):

The presentation should include:

  • Problem statement
  • Design approach
  • Key UI/UX decisions
  • Final product concept

Optional Design Documentation:

A short report describing:

  • Challenges faced
  • Design iterations
  • Implementation feasibility

All submissions must be uploaded before the announced deadline.

Judging Criteria:

  • Innovation & Creativity: 25%
  • Technical Implementation: 25%
  • Feasibility & Accuracy: 20%
  • Presentation & Communication: 15%
  • Impact / Relevance: 15%

Scoring Overview:

  • User Experience & Usability: 50 Points
  • UI / Visual Design Quality: 25 Points
  • Design Feasibility: 15 Points
  • Innovation & Aesthetics: 10 Points

Total Score: 100 Points

Penalties And Disqualification:

  • Late Submission: −10 Points
  • Use of Templates or UI Kits: −20 Points or Disqualification
  • Incomplete Submission: −20 Points
  • Rule Violations: −10 to −20 Points
  • Misconduct or Plagiarism: Immediate Disqualification

Awards And Recognition:

  • 1st Prize: ₹20,000
  • 2nd Prize: ₹15,000
  • 3rd Prize: ₹10,000

All participants will receive International Participation Certificates.

Official Notice:

This rule book serves as the official international guideline for the Pixel Craft UI/UX Design Challenge under INNOVATEX 4.0 at Presidency University, Bengaluru.

All decisions made by the jury and organizing committee are final and binding.

data.title: Mini Smart India Hackathon 2026
data.details:
Event Overview:

  • Mini Smart India Hackathon 2026 (Mini-SIH) is a 24-hour innovation hackathon organized by InnovateX at Presidency University, Bengaluru.
  • Inspired by the national-level Smart India Hackathon, this event brings the same spirit of innovation, collaboration, and real-world problem-solving to the university level.
  • Teams will compete to build impactful solutions across multiple tracks by solving real-world challenges within 24 hours.

Event Details:

  • Grand Finale Date: 6 April 2026 (11:00 AM) – 7 April 2026 (11:00 AM)
  • Venue: Presidency University, Bengaluru (Offline)
  • Duration: 24-Hour Non-Stop Hackathon
  • Team Size: Exactly 4 Members
  • Theme: Open Innovation – Any Domain

Registration Information:

  • Registration Period: 3 March 2026 – 27 March 2026

Registration Process:

Step 1: Register and Pay on Unstop

  • External participants must complete the ₹1,200 registration payment through the Unstop payment gateway.
  • Payment on Unstop alone does not complete team registration.

Step 2: Complete Registration on the Official Portal

  • After payment, teams must register on the official Mini-SIH portal.
  • Required details during portal registration:
  • Payment transaction ID
  • Payment screenshot proof
  • Complete details of all team members
  • Failure to complete portal registration may lead to disqualification even if payment is made.

Tracks:

Software Edition

  • Develop apps, websites, AI/ML models, platforms, or any software-based solution addressing real-world problems.

Hardware Edition

  • Build hardware prototypes such as IoT devices, robotics systems, embedded solutions, circuits, and more.

Student Innovation

  • An open category encouraging creative ideas, including social innovation, business models, design solutions, and interdisciplinary problem-solving.

Awards And Prizes:

  • Total Prize Pool: ₹45,000

Software Edition:

  • Winner – ₹10,000+
  • 1st Runner-Up – ₹5,000+
  • 2nd Runner-Up – Certificate of Merit

Hardware Edition

  • Winner – ₹10,000+
  • 1st Runner-Up – ₹5,000+
  • 2nd Runner-Up – Certificate of Merit

Student Innovation

  • Winner – ₹10,000+
  • 1st Runner-Up – ₹5,000+
  • 2nd Runner-Up – Certificate of Merit
  • All Grand Finalists receive a Grand Finale Certificate and Recognition.
  • All participants receive an Official Participation Certificate.

Why Participate In Mini-SIH:

  • Opportunity to win cash prizes and recognized certificates.
  • Gain hands-on experience solving real-world challenges within 24 hours.
  • Network with students, mentors, and innovators.
  • Experience the Smart India Hackathon-style competition environment.
  • Build a strong project for your portfolio or resume.
  • Receive mentorship from industry experts during the hackathon.

Key Highlights:

  • 24-hour non-stop offline hackathon.
  • Total prize pool of ₹45,000.
  • Three competition tracks: Software, Hardware, and Student Innovation.
  • Certificates were provided to all participants.
  • The open innovation theme allows ideas from any domain.
  • Mentorship support throughout the hackathon.

prizes.others: Prize Type: Cash Amount: ₹10,000 Perks: Winner Certificate + Grand Finale Recognition + Official Participation Certificate
Prize Type: Cash Amount: ₹5,000 Perks: Runner-Up Certificate + Official Participation Certificate

data.title: National Road Safety Hackathon 2026
data.details:
The National Road Safety Hackathon 2026, organised by the Centre of Excellence for Road Safety (CoERS), RBG Labs, IIT Madras, is focused on the theme “AI in Road Safety”, aiming to encourage the development of innovative, technology-driven solutions to improve road safety through artificial intelligence. Participants are invited to attempt any one of the three topics:

  1. DriveLegal:
    • This tool provides location-specific information on traffic laws, violations, and fine schedules, integrating national rules with state and local enforcement regulations to improve compliance with road safety rules. 
  2. RoadWatch:
    • The tool enables citizens to monitor road quality, track public spending, and report issues to the responsible authorities, increasing the transparency in road infrastructure. 
  3. RoadSoS:
    • The tool provides location-based access to nearby trauma centres, ambulance services, vehicle rescue services, police stations and emergency contacts during road accidents. 

Eligibility Criteria:

  1. All students, engineers, and problem solvers enthusiastic about ethics in technology are eligible to participate. No professional qualifications are necessary.
  2. Minimum age for participation is 15

Team Composition: 

  1. Participation is open to individuals as well as teams.
  2. There is no restriction on the number of participants in a team; individuals or groups of any size may take part in the hackathon. 

Awards:

The winners will receive the following cash prizes, and all participants who successfully clear Stage 1 and submit their entries will be awarded a participation certificate. Selected participants may also have the opportunity to receive job or internship offers. 

  • 1st Prize - ₹ 50,000 
  • 2nd Prize - ₹ 30,000 
  • 3rd Prize - ₹ 20,000 

Competition Timelines:

  • Registration and submissions open on 11th March 2026 
  • Registrations ends by 15th April 2026 
  • Submissions close on 31st May 2026 

Competition Guidelines:

Stage 1:

  • Participants/ Teams can submit their solutions through code in any programming language of their choice 
  • They are also required to submit a presentation consisting of 7 slides, including “Welcome” and “Thank you” explaining their solution.
  • The participants need to share the entire code and relevant details like the list of software packages used and assumptions as a word document.   
  • Registrations and Submissions are only accepted through Unstop 
  • Participants and teams alike can make only one submission 
  • A team can make only one submission out of the 3-topic explained above 

Stage 2:

Shortlisted teams will present their solution to a live jury at the IITM campus. The exact date and schedule of the presentation will be intimated to the qualifying teams 

Kindly refer to the link below to access the National Road Safety Hackathon 2026 Rule Book.

https://coers.iitm.ac.in/events/Hackathon/2026/rule_book

data.title: Hack Odyssey Test
data.details:
• Hack Odyssey 3.0 is a 24-hour Inter-College Hackathon organized by the KARE ACM Student Chapter and KARE ACM-W Student Chapter, Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Kalasalingam Academy of Research and Education.

  • The event brings together students from different institutions to collaborate, innovate, and develop solutions to real-world problems.

  • Participants will build innovative prototypes aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) such as healthcare, sustainable agriculture, education, clean water, and climate action.

  • The hackathon provides a platform for students to showcase their technical skills, creativity, and teamwork while working under a 24-hour innovation challenge.

  • Industry experts and academicians will evaluate the projects and provide valuable feedback, mentorship, and guidance to the participants.

data.title: Hack Hustle 2.0
data.details:
A 24-hour offline innovation marathon to build solutions that secure our future. Join 300+ developers at Saveetha Engineering College on April 16 & 17, 2026

The Stakes (2L+):
We’re putting a serious prize pool on the line for the sharpest minds:

  • Winners: ₹20,000 Cash 
  • Runners-up:  ₹15,000 Cash

The Secret Files: We have Special Category Prizes for Best Innovation, Cleanest Code, and more. We’re keeping these under wraps for now... let’s just say you’ll want to bring your A-game.

Whether you’re a seasoned architect or a first-time coder, this is your chance to tackle real-world challenges, collaborate with the best, and revolutionize the tech landscape.
